questions.explanation: git checkout -b <branch> creates the branch and checks it out in a single command.
questions.question: In Git, which command creates a new local branch named 'feature/login' and switches to it in one step?

questions.explanation: Build tools handle dependency resolution and automate tasks like compiling, packaging, and running tests.

options.A: It records UI interactions to generate tests automatically
options.B: It manages dependencies and automates build/test tasks
options.C: It replaces the need for a version control system
options.D: It runs tests only in a browser without any configuration

questions.question: Which statement best describes the purpose of a build tool like Maven/Gradle/npm in an automation project?

questions.explanation: A unique id is typically the most stable and fastest locator compared to brittle absolute XPaths or generic tags.
questions.question: In Selenium WebDriver, which locator strategy is generally the most stable when the application provides unique identifiers?

questions.explanation: A pipeline triggered on push/merge ensures tests run automatically and consistently for each change.

options.A: Running tests only on developer machines before merging
options.B: Triggering a pipeline on each push/merge to main
options.D: Storing test results only as screenshots on a shared drive

questions.question: Which CI/CD practice best helps ensure automated tests run consistently on every change to the main branch?

questions.explanation: POM separates page-specific details (locators/actions) from test logic, improving maintainability and reuse.

options.A: Store all locators and test assertions in a single test class for simplicity
options.B: Create a class per page/screen that exposes actions and element accessors, while tests focus on business flows
options.C: Put API calls and UI locators in the same utility class to reduce files
options.D: Generate tests by recording user actions and never refactor them

questions.question: You want to implement the Page Object Model (POM). Which design best matches POM principles?
questions.topic: Test Design & Automation Patterns (Page Object Model, Data-Driven)

questions.explanation: @pytest.mark.parametrize is the standard pytest feature for data-driven tests, producing separate test cases per dataset.

options.A: Use @pytest.mark.parametrize to supply multiple sets of arguments
options.B: Duplicate the test function once per dataset
options.C: Hard-code all datasets inside the test body as a single string
options.D: Use a try/except block to loop until one dataset passes

questions.question: In pytest, which approach is most appropriate for running the same test with multiple input datasets (data-driven testing)?

questions.explanation: Robust API tests validate protocol-level correctness (status), structure (schema/fields), and business correctness (key values).

options.A: Only check that the response time is under 5 seconds
options.B: Only check that the response body is not empty
options.C: Check status code, validate JSON schema/required fields, and assert key business values (e.g., orderId not null)
options.D: Only check that the response contains the word 'success'

questions.question: You are testing a REST API endpoint POST /orders that returns 201 and a JSON body. Which validation is most valuable for a robust automated API test?

questions.explanation: Explicit waits synchronize on real UI conditions, reducing flakiness compared to fixed sleeps that are either too short or waste time.

options.A: Replace all waits with Thread.sleep(5000) everywhere
options.B: Use explicit waits for specific conditions (e.g., element visible/clickable) and remove arbitrary sleeps
options.C: Run the pipeline only at night when fewer users are online
options.D: Disable parallel execution so tests run slower but unchanged

questions.question: Your UI tests in GitHub Actions are flaky because elements sometimes load slowly. Which change most directly improves reliability without adding excessive fixed delays?

questions.explanation: Modular components with defined interfaces, fail-safe behavior, and logging improve safety, maintainability, and diagnosability in automation systems.

options.A: Implement all logic in one large script so it is easy to deploy as a single file
options.B: Use a modular design with clear interfaces (sensor input module, control logic module, actuator output module), include fail-safe states, and log events
options.C: Rely on manual operator intervention instead of implementing interlocks
options.D: Remove logging to maximize performance and avoid disk usage

questions.question: You need to design automation components for a production line where a sensor triggers an actuator. The system must be safe and maintainable. Which approach is best?

questions.explanation: Using version control, documentation, version pinning, and CI artifact retention provides auditability, reproducibility, and traceable evidence.

options.A: Modify the tool directly in production without version control to save time
options.B: Fork the tool/config in Git, document changes, pin versions, and integrate report artifacts into CI with retained history
options.C: Disable report generation to avoid storing test evidence
options.D: Only keep the latest report and delete previous ones to reduce storage

questions.question: You are asked to operate and customize an open-source test reporting tool (e.g., Allure) for a regulated environment. Which action best supports traceability and compliance?

topics_covered: Test Design & Automation Patterns (Page Object Model, Data-Driven)
